candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line shown separately at the same level as the selected COGS rows and SG&A rows. The duplicate detail row pair indicates a summary structure where total Depreciation is split into Depreciation and Depreciation right-of-use assets components; to avoid double counting, use only the summary total. In this shipping operating statement, the separate depreciation line most likely relates primarily to operating asset costs rather than being embedded in the selected COGS rows.
Looks like a component of the depreciation total because it approximately sums with the other Depreciation row to the top Depreciation summary. Should not be chosen if the summary row is used.
Adjustment row is not a core D&A expense row to add separately and could distort the expense mapping.
Balancing value is not a substantive D&A expense row for separate addition.

variables.notes: OK. Selected the summary operating D&A row because explicit depreciation is presented separately from the selected COGS bucket rows, so it does not appear embedded in those chosen COGS lines. The summary row is preferred over component rows to avoid double counting.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line exists, but it is generic and not identified as SG&A-related. With no explicit SG&A D&A row such as depreciation in SGA/admin, adding it to XSGA would risk misclassification and double counting against COGS-oriented operating depreciation.
Appears to be a component of the total depreciation row rather than a standalone SG&A D&A row.
Adjustment row is not an SG&A-specific D&A expense row for separate addition.
Balancing value is not a substantive SG&A D&A expense row for separate addition.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A-specific depreciation/amortization row appears in the excerpt. The visible depreciation lines are generic operating rows and are more plausibly associated with operating asset costs than with the selected SG&A bucket. Conservative choice is to add nothing separately to XSGA.
